{"id":"project-LICHEN-tm1t","title":"coap_status.c: format_ipv6 does not use standard zero compression","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/coap/coap_status.c
Lines: 131-141

The IPv6 formatting function outputs addresses in non-standard format:
```c
return snprintf(buf, buf_size,
        "%02x%02x:%02x%02x:%02x%02x:%02x%02x:"
        "%02x%02x:%02x%02x:%02x%02x:%02x%02x", ...);
```

This outputs "fe80:0000:0000:0000:0211:22ff:fe33:4455" instead of the standard "fe80::211:22ff:fe33:4455".

While technically valid, this:
1. Uses more bytes than necessary (increases CBOR size)
2. Does not match the format used by net_addr_ntop in coap_msg.c
3. May confuse clients expecting standard format

Category: PROTOCOL - non-standard address format","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:44:20.489211Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:44:20.489248Z"}
